subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A grease interceptor must contain a permanently fixed, ridged baffle constructed of a minimum 6 mm—

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A grease interceptor must be installed on a minimum of 75 mm compacted packing sand.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A grease interceptor must have a minimum DN 50 vent where airtight covers are fitted.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A grease interceptor installed underground must be constructed with—

ol.li: minimum 100 mm concrete walls with a smooth internal finish; and
have suitable reinforcing mesh within the concrete.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A grease interceptor installed above ground must—

ol.li: be constructed of an authorised material; and

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The capacity of a grease interceptor must be authorised by the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A grease interceptor must be installed in a location that will facilitate ready access for maintenance and inspection.

callout.p: Where the Permit Authority has not specified the capacity of a grease interceptor in accordance with C4D12(5), it must be—

ol.li: equivalent to the maximum hourly discharge; and
no less than 250 litres below the outlet.
The contents of a grease interceptor may become slightly acidic. It is recommended that the internal concrete surfaces below the outlet level be provided with an acidic resistant lining.
Where the grease interceptor is installed outside a building, consideration should be given to fitting airtight covers.
See TAS Figure C4D12 for a typical grease interceptor for above ground installations.
